trivia_bullets.text: Modern Horizons 3 (released June 14, 2024) made Energy its headliner mechanic — the first time Energy had returned as a prominent draft theme since Kaladesh/Aether Revolt (2016-2017). MH3 featured an Energy-themed Commander precon deck ('Creative Energy') and seeded energy producers throughout the draft environment. Bespoke Battlewagon slots into this as a blue Artifact Vehicle that generates two energy per tap and converts stored energy into creature-tap, card draw, or self-animation, making it a multi-role energy sink. [Scryfall: set_type draft_innovation; EDHREC: Creative Energy Precon Review MH3]
sources: EDHREC: Creative Energy Precon Review Modern Horizons 3

trivia_bullets.text: Artist credit confirmed via Scryfall: Zoltan Boros (solo credit). Bespoke Battlewagon is one of five unique card illustrations Zoltan Boros contributed to MH3 — the others are Galvanic Discharge (MH3 no. 122), Sevinne's Reclamation (MH3 no. 267), White Orchid Phantom (MH3 no. 47), and a retro-frame variant of Eladamri, Korvecdal (MH3 no. 423). Boros worked as half of the 'Boros-Szikszai' illustration partnership with Gabor Szikszai for approximately 25 years (1986-2010) before the formal collaboration ended when Boros relocated to New York State; all five MH3 credits are solo Boros work. The Boros-Szikszai partnership shared a studio site at boros-szikszai.com and contributed extensively to MTG, D&D, Star Wars RPG, and World of Warcraft TCG. [Scryfall: artist Zoltan Boros, MH3 artist query; Boros-Szikszai.com partnership history]

Bespoke Battlewagon holds an EDHREC rank of approximately 6,100, the strongest Commander showing of the four MH3 cards in this batch, reflecting its adoption in energy-focused Commander decks. EDHREC data shows it appearing frequently alongside energy-centric commanders including Satya, Aetherflux Genius and Iron Man, Titan of Innovation (Universes Beyond). The card was previewed by pro player Javier Dominguez on his Twitter/X account (May 28, 2024) [Scryfall: preview source Javier Dominguez]. It was reprinted in the Aetherdrift Commander set (DRC no. 71, released February 14, 2025) in the Living Energy Commander deck, confirming its recognized role as a supporting energy engine. [Scryfall DRC reprint; Scryfall: EDHREC rank; EDHREC card page]
